channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: An AI-powered hotel is coming to Las Vegas
item.description: Artificial intelligence was everywhere at CES in 2025, but in the coming months, it’s going to be a more permanent part of the Las Vegas landscape. This March will see the soft opening of the Otonomus Hotel, which strives to be different than your typical Vegas hotel room or suite.
item.dc:creator: Chris Morris, Fast Company

item.title: Next-gen Ethernet standards set to move forward in 2025
item.description: 2024 was a banner year for Ethernet, which has been a foundational element of networking for more than 50 years and is poised for more advancements as 2025 kicks off. Multiple standards and certification efforts made progress in 2024, helping networks to be more reliable and deliver more bandwidth.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Senate Democrat plans legislation to delay TikTok ban
item.description: Sen. Ed Markey (D-Mass.) said Monday he is planning to introduce legislation to give the Chinese parent company of TikTok more time to sell the app as the ban deadline looms less than a week away.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: The Nintendo Sound Clock: Alarmo is coming to participating retailers in March 2025, no Switch Online membership required
item.description: Nintendo has announced that its Sound Clock: Alarmo will be coming to participating retailers in March 2025.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Trump says he had 'very good experience with TikTok' as court considers ban
item.description: President-elect Trump said Monday that he had a “very good experience with TikTok” as he awaits the Supreme Court’s decision on a law that could ban the popular video-sharing platform starting Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: JPMorgan tells all its employees they must return to office full-time
item.description: All of JPMorgan Chase’s workers called back to the office full-time as the company turns its back on hybrid working.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Biden signs executive order aimed at growing AI infrastructure in the US
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — President Joe Biden on Tuesday signed an ambitious executive order on artificial intelligence that seeks to ensure the infrastructure needed for advanced AI operations, such as large-scale data centers and new clean power facilities, can be built quickly and at scale in the United
item.dc:creator: SARAH PARVINI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Google faces more scrutiny as UK watchdog flexes new digital competition powers
item.description: Britain’s competition watchdog says it’s investigating Google’s search ad business, adding to global scrutiny that the U.S. tech giant is facing.
item.dc:creator: Kelvin Chan, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Digital Workplace en la banca: análisis de soluciones para un entorno más ágil y seguro
item.description: El sector bancario atraviesa una etapa de transformación tecnológica orientada a responder con mayor eficiencia a los desafíos presentes y futuros.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Microsoft claims its servers were illegally accessed to make unsafe AI content
item.description: Unsurprisingly, a scheme which involved developing tools to steal API keys and access Microsoft servers without permission isn’t faring well in a Virginia court.
item.dc:creator: luke.hughes@futurenet.com (Luke Hughes), TechRadar

item.title: Microsoft component retirement frenzy geared to product simplification: Analyst
item.description: A rash of planned feature and application retirements within the Microsoft 365 environment is the result of Microsoft wanting to centralize, modernize, operationalize customer spend, and ultimately economize by simplifying product offerings where it can, an industry analyst said Monday.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Britain wants to become an AI powerhouse. Here’s how
item.description: The British government pledged on Monday to rip up the red tape hindering construction of data centers that underpin artificial intelligence while promoting its “pro-innovation” approach to regulating the technology.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: TikTok case: 4 takeaways from the Supreme Court arguments
item.description: Congress enacted the law out of concern that the China-based app is susceptible to the influence of the Chinese government and posed a national risk.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times News Service Syndicate, The Mercury News

item.title: ClickUp CEO Zeb Evans on why workplaces will be using fewer platforms in the future
item.description: If your team subscribes to 10 different software tools, you’re not alone in wrestling with the overwhelming sprawl of productivity apps. Entrepreneurs have taken notice.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Caplan, Fast Company

item.title: Unauthorized drone flights double; 3 arrested during LA fires
item.description: The FAA instituted temporary flight restrictions in the area. Aircraft are used to drop water on the blazes.
item.dc:creator: Steph Whiteside, NewsNation

item.title: Microsoft's own baddie team 'attacked' more than 100 generative AI products: Here's what they learnt
item.description: Microsoft's red team uncovers AI vulnerabilities by thinking like adversaries to strengthen generative AI systems.
item.dc:creator: udinmwenefosa@gmail.com (Efosa Udinmwen), TechRadar

item.title: Britain's leader unveils AI strategy, including vow to rip up red tape hindering AI projects
item.description: LONDON (AP) — The British government pledged on Monday to rip up the red tape hindering construction of data centers that underpin artificial intelligence while promoting its “pro-innovation” approach to regulating the technology.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Adobe’s Firefly ‘Bulk Create’ lets users edit thousands of images at once
item.description: Adobe has unveiled a new Firefly “bulk create” feature that lets content designers make changes to thousands of images once, automating actions such as resizing assets and replacing backgrounds at scale.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: The devops certifications tech companies want
item.description: Devops continues to expand in development environments everywhere from small startups to the largest global enterprises.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: This free tool transforms Chrome into an on-demand scratchpad
item.description: I’ve tried a lot of fancy note-taking apps, but I find myself constantly coming back to the classic old Windows Notepad app on my PC. Sometimes, I even just jot things down on paper. As someone who’s constantly juggling a jillion different things, there’s always a lot to keep track of.
item.dc:creator: Chris Hoffman, Fast Company

item.title: A young gorilla rescued from aircraft hold recovers at Istanbul zoo
item.description: A young gorilla rescued from a plane’s cargo hold is recovering at an Istanbul zoo while wildlife officers consider returning him to his natural habitat.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: CES was a giant exercise in AI gaslighting
item.description: For a reporter covering CES, the booth for Samsung Display can seem like a pure distillation of what the tech trade show is all about.
item.dc:creator: Jared Newman, Fast Company

item.title: How AI is helping an Israeli TV reporter speak clearly again
item.description: When a renowned Israeli TV journalist lost his ability to speak clearly because of ALS, he thought his career might be over.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: For 2025, the return of IT efficiency and effectiveness
item.description: Current and former executives say the federal technology themes for 2025 will be similar to 2024 with the addition of efficiency and effectiveness. The post For 2025, the return of IT efficiency and effectiveness first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jason Miller, Federal News Network

item.title: Prime Video's use of AI, Next Gen Stats on NFL games is helping viewers understand the game better
item.description: CULVER CITY, Calif. (AP) — The Los Angeles Rams defense appeared to be content to drop back in coverage and prevent San Francisco’s Brock Purdy from completing a Hail Mary on the final play of their Thursday night game on Dec. 12. The broadcast on Amazon Prime Video saw it differently, though.
item.dc:creator: JOE REEDY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: TikTok gets frosty reception at Supreme Court in fight to stave off ban
item.description: TikTok received a frosty reception in its fight to save the platform at the Supreme Court, which during oral arguments Friday expressed sympathy with the government’s national security concerns about the platform’s ties to China.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, NewsNation

item.title: Tech unemployment in the US drops to lowest level in more than two years
item.description: Tech hiring rose in December, dropping the IT unemployment rate to 2% — its lowest since November 2023, according to an analysis of the latest jobs data published today by the US Bureau of Labor statistics (BLS). The overall national unemployment rate held steady at 4.1%, according to the BLS.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Ephemeral environments in cloud-native development
item.description: An emerging trend in cloud computing is using ephemeral environments for development and testing. Ephemeral environments are temporary, isolated spaces created for specific projects.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: The Future of In-Vehicle Experiences: CES 2024 Highlights - AP News
item.description: Hyundai Mobis, BMW, Honda, and Sony Honda Mobility showcased their latest advances for in-vehicle experiences at CES 2024.
item.dc:creator: WYATTE GRANTHAM-PHILIPS, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Meta tries letting Facebook Marketplace users view eBay listings as way to resolve EU charges
item.description: Meta said Wednesday that it will allow some Facebook users to view eBay listings on its Marketplace service, as it tries out a possible way to resolve European Union charges of anticompetitive behavior that the bloc leveled last year.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: CES 2023: Worst in Show Awards Go to Products with Surveillance Tech, Excessive Waste, and Security Risks
item.description: CES 2023: Worst in Show Awards Go to Products with Surveillance Tech, Excessive Waste, and Security Risks
item.dc:creator: Sarah Parvini, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Meta rolls back hate speech rules as Zuckerberg cites 'recent elections' as a catalyst
item.description: It wasn't just fact-checking that Meta scrapped from its platforms as it prepares for the second Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: BARBARA ORTUTAY, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Federal News Network’s AI & Data Exchange 2025
item.description: AI in action: How agencies are building out trusted artificial intelligence capabilities The post Federal News Network’s AI & Data Exchange 2025 first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: wfedstaff, Federal News Network

item.title: Cisco in 2025: Lots of hard work ahead
item.description: For a company of its size, Cisco executed a fairly short list of technology rollouts in 2024 and made a surprising lack of major updates to its core networking portfolio, which might have contributed to the 23% decline in networking revenue Cisco reported in its latest quarterly financials.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Closing the digital gap: The importance of secure development practices in government IT modernization
item.description: In today's fast-paced digital landscape, effective government IT modernization is crucial for agencies to achieve their missions. The post Closing the digital gap: The importance of secure development practices in government IT modernization first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: commentators, Federal News Network

item.title: Nvidia launches blueprints to help jump start AI projects
item.description: Give Nvidia credit, it’s not just talking up big ideas, it’s helping its customers achieve them. The vendor recently issued designs for AI factories after hyping up the idea for several months.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: ByteDance's Lemon8 gains traction amid TikTok ban threat as creators push the app
item.description: Hearing a lot about Lemon8 lately? You’re not the only one. Amid a looming U.S. ban on TikTok, content creators have been pushing the platform's sister app.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Meta eliminates fact-checking in latest bow to Trump
item.description: Meta chief executive officer Mark Zuckerberg on Tuesday announced changes to content moderation on Facebook and Instagram long sought by conservatives. Incoming President Donald Trump said the new approach was “probably” due to threats he made against the technology mogul.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, BARBARA ORTUTAY and NICHOLAS RICCARDI, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Facebook to end fact-checking, boost political content
item.description: The company said automated moderation would focus on serious issues like terrorism and child sexual exploitation instead.
item.dc:creator: Steph Whiteside, NewsNation
